PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Dec-03-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Bresler, Esq., counsel for Elaine Tan, Administrator (appearing by video conference); Lawrence Siracusa, Esq., Guardian ad litem for Jordan Sun and Jack Sun, Minors (appearing by video conference).

The Court initially queries Mr. Bresler as to a time itemization of the fees claimed for Mr. Launay who reports that the requested itemization was filed over one year ago. The Court notes that the retention of Mr. Launay was not authorized by the Court. The Court further questions Mr. Bresler as to his understanding of what activities would constitute fees within the scope of "statutory fees" and those within the scope of "extraordinary fees."
Mr. Bresler explains the scope of the services provided by Mr. Launay, including landlord/tenant litigation and quiet title litigation. Court and counsel discuss the efficaciousness of those efforts, including Mr. Bresler's participation in the same.
Court and counsel, including Mr. Siracusa, discuss the status of the bonding for the Administrator, concluding that there is not sufficient bond at this time and that the same must be remedied as soon as possible.

At the Court's invitation, Mr. Sircusa notes that the fees requested appear to be excessive for the results produced, that the request is premature as these fees are usually requested at the conclusion of the estate proceeding, and that there appears to be no impediment to proceeding with a Petition for Final Distribution. He further notes that it is not clear what the assets of the estate are from the petition before the Court.

The Court advises Mr. Bresler that it will be preparing an Order setting forth its schedule going forward, but with the understanding that a Petition for Final Distribution will be required, and its disposition of the matter now pending before the Court. The Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Deputy: K. Kabasares; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Bresler for (administrator) Elaine Tan. Christina Chen for and with (interested party) Mary Sun.

Mr. Bresler reported that he has been unable to contact his client over the holiday and has made no progress toward the tentative ruling. Ms. Chen stated that there are two petitions on calendar, that they would like a separate hearing, that this administration is a long way from closing, that they would like a professional Guardian ad Litem (GAL) appointed, that there is currently a petition to appoint Steven Lee, that they object to the appointment stating that Mr. Lee is not impartial and is a cousin to the administrator citing CCP 374, that this hearing has been continued 4 times and that the civil matter keeps getting continued to trail this one. The Court asked if it were to sever the petitions would the refinancing be severed out. Mr. Bresler stated that it cannot be severed, that this is a statute of limitations case, that opposing counsel did not bring in representation for the children, that Mr. Lee is a relative, that they cannot establish divergence and that he is willing to ask his client to agree to a private fiduciary. The Court stated that it is inclined to accept a neutral GAL and that if there is no agreement by 1/6/16 the court will appoint one and asked about the refinancing. Mr. Bresler stated that they oppose the refinancing and that the interests of the children are not benefitted by it. The Court asked what will happen if the GAL recommends refinancing. Ms. Chen stated that the statute of limitations should have been raised in the civil issue not here, that the need for a GAL should have been raised by the administrator 4 years ago and that no attention has been paid to the minor's rights until now. The Court asked what prejudice would there be in a delay and why not wait. Ms. Chen stated that waiting will lend itself to the administrators Modis Operandi, that the administrator is changing her mind regarding the settlement agreement and that she is playing a game. The Court stated that it will think about the severance. Mr. Bresler stated that they do not think this is a game and that it is a statute of limitations case. The accounting petition and the petition for order approving settlement agreement and general release, etc., were continued to February 22, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the GAL's report regarding the settlement agreement and that if a recommendation for a neutral GAL is not received by 1/6/16 the court will appoint one.
